summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened this Easter Sunday message by celebrating the resurrection of Jesus and welcoming the congregation. He shared his personal testimony of being arrested for a DUI as a teenager and how that difficult situation led his mother and pastors to lead him in a prayer to receive Jesus Christ as his Lord and Savior. From that moment, his life was forever changed. The core of the sermon was an exploration of what it means to be 'saved by grace.' Pastor Travis explained that this salvation is a free gift from God, paid for by the death of Jesus on the cross, not something we can earn through good deeds. He outlined four key areas from which God's grace saves us. First, it saves us from our sins, which he defined as 'missing the mark' of God's perfection. Second, it saves us from ourselves, our own human tendencies and struggles, using the Apostle Paul's candid confession in Romans 7 as an example. Third, God's grace saves us from our difficult situations, even those we create ourselves, referencing Paul's 'thorn in the flesh' as a testament to God's sufficient grace in hardship. Finally, and most importantly, God's grace saves us from eternal separation from Him, offering the free gift of eternal life. The message concluded with a powerful invitation for anyone unsure of their salvation to pray and surrender their life to Jesus, resulting in many responding. Pastor Travis emphasized that in Christ, we become a new creation, and encouraged everyone to share their decision.
mainPoints: God's grace saves me from my sins, a free gift paid for by Jesus on the cross, not by our own good works.
God's grace saves me from myself, delivering me from my human struggles and tendencies, as illustrated by the Apostle Paul's honesty.
God's grace saves me from my situations and from eternal separation, offering sufficient strength for life's trials and the promise of heaven.

versesMentioned.text: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God’s grace
in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.

I do not understand what I do. For what I want to do I do not do, but what I hate I do. And if I do what I do not want to do, I agree that the law is good. As it is, it is no longer I myself who do it, but it is sin living in me. For I know that good itself does not dwell in me, that is, in my sinful nature. For I have the desire to do what is good, but I cannot carry it out. For I do not do the good I want to do, but the evil I do not want to do—this I keep on doing.
What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me from this body that is subject to death? Thanks be to God, who delivers me through Jesus Christ our Lord!
Is anyone among you in trouble? Let them pray. Is anyone happy? Let them sing songs of praise.
and call on me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you will honor me.

or because of these surpassingly great revelations. Therefore, in order to keep me from becoming conceited, I was given a thorn in my flesh, a messenger of Satan, to torment me. Three times I pleaded with the Lord to take it away from me. But he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.
For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
But because of his great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy, made us alive with Christ even when we were dead in transgressions—it is by grace you have been saved.
If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Ephesians 2:4-5 (NIV) - 'But because of his great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy, made us alive with Christ even when we were dead in transgressions—it is by grace you have been saved.'
Monday.focus: The foundation of salvation is God's love and grace, not our works.
Monday.action: Begin your day by thanking God that your standing with Him is based on His grace, not your performance.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for your great love and rich mercy. Help me to rest in the truth that I am saved by your grace alone today.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 7:24-25 (NIV) - 'What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me...? Thanks be to God, who delivers me through Jesus Christ our Lord!'
Tuesday.focus: God's grace saves us from our own internal struggles.
Tuesday.action: Identify one personal struggle or habit. Instead of just trying harder, pray and ask God for His delivering grace in that area.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I confess my struggle with [name it]. I cannot overcome this on my own. Thank you for delivering me through Jesus. I rely on your grace today.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'But he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.”'
Wednesday.focus: God's grace is sufficient for our weaknesses and difficult situations.
Wednesday.action: Think of a current challenge or 'thorn.' Write down the phrase 'His grace is sufficient' and place it where you'll see it as a reminder.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, my situation feels heavy. I bring my weakness and my trouble to you. I choose to believe your promise that your grace is all I need to get through this.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 6:23 (NIV) - 'For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.'
Thursday.focus: The eternal consequence of sin and the free gift of salvation.
Thursday.action: Reflect on the incredible gift of eternal life. If you've received it, tell one person today why you're thankful for Jesus. If you haven't, consider taking that step.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for paying the price for my sin. Thank you for the free gift of eternal life. Help me to live today in light of this amazing truth.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 50:15 (NIV) - 'and call on me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you will honor me.'
Friday.focus: God invites us to call on Him in trouble, promising deliverance.
Friday.action: Practice turning to prayer first when a problem or worry arises today, before trying to solve it on your own.
Friday.prayer: God, I call on you now with the troubles on my heart. I trust you to deliver me and guide me. I want to honor you through this situation.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 10:9 (NIV) - 'If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.'
Saturday.focus: The simplicity of salvation: belief and confession.
Saturday.action: If you made a decision to follow Jesus this week, tell a trusted Christian friend or family member about it. If you've been a believer for years, reaffirm your faith by sharing your story with someone.
Saturday.prayer: Lord Jesus, I believe in my heart that you are Lord and that God raised you from the dead. I declare with my mouth that you are my Savior. Thank you for saving me.
